body.title: [triage] 150 sprint tickets carry NO intention — so under today's ruling they cannot be proven. There is no property to break.
body.intention: I intend that no ticket enters sprint without an intention, because the intention IS the property mutation-red breaks — so a ticket without one cannot be proven, and under 'every ticket has a proof, period' it cannot honestly close either. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**MEASURED 2026-07-13:** 2,376 tickets. 456 carry an intention. 202 LIVE tickets carry none — and **150 of those are in `sprint`**: approved, queued, waiting to be built. (The other 1,718 missing ones are closed history — archaeology, explicitly out of scope.)

**WHY THIS IS URGENT NOW AND WAS NOT YESTERDAY.** Two things ratified 2026-07-13 collide with it:
1. Akien: 'every ticket needs a proof. period.' No discriminator, no exemption class.
2. Already in the corpus: 'You must know the intention to know what to break. Mutation needs the property, and the property IS the intention — so intention -> mutation -> red.'

Together: **a ticket with no intention has no property to break, therefore no mutation-red, therefore no proof.** Those 150 cannot satisfy the close gate as they stand. They will each arrive at close time and face a choice between a fake proof and a shipped-unproven that names a proof method we DO have — which is the dishonest use of the label. This is the 'closed PASS on three empty files' failure (already recorded in the outline), pre-loaded 150 times.

**IT IS ALSO THE PRESSURE THAT MAKES FABRICATION TEMPTING.** T-intent-extractor-agent originally proposed inferring these from the code. That is CUT (circular: inferring the property from the implementation and then testing the implementation against it passes on any implementation, including a hollow one). So the 150 must be SOLVED honestly, not inferred away — and refusing the shortcut is only legitimate if the real path is actually walked.

**THE HONEST PATH — escalation is spec-quality data.** A missing intention is a QUESTION FOR THE HUMAN, not a hole for a model to fill. But Akien should not face 150 blank pages (CP4): the extractor DRAFTS a candidate, the human RATIFIES or rewrites, and only the ratified sentence is written. Draft-then-review, which is Akien's own standing preference. An unratified draft never reaches the field.

**THE STRUCTURAL FIX (so this never re-accumulates):** an intention becomes a PRECONDITION OF ENTERING SPRINT, enforced at the status transition in cc_queue.py — not a nag at close time, when it is far too late to be honest about it. `cc_queue.py add` already audits for a missing `intention:` field (it caught CC filing one without it, 2026-07-13) — but that audit does not GATE, and nothing gates the triage->sprint transition. **A check that warns but does not gate is how 150 accumulated.**

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (gate the ->sprint transition; the `add` audit already exists but does not block), devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/*.json (the 150), skills/ticket + skills/sprint (authoring path must ask).

**Design rules:** every-ticket-has-a-proof (Akien 2026-07-13); intention->mutation->red; escalation-is-spec-quality-data (missing intention -> ask the human, never fabricate); draft-then-review (CP4 — do not hand Akien 150 blank pages); CP1 (an absent intention is honestly absent; an invented one is a confident wrong answer at the point where deduction was supposed to stop).

**Scope boundary:** IN — the sprint-entry gate; surfacing the 150 as a worklist; the draft->ratify path for clearing them. OUT — backfilling the 1,718 CLOSED tickets (archaeology); building the extractor itself (T-intent-extractor-agent owns the drafter and the validator); authoring the 150 sentences (that is Akien's, one review at a time — this ticket makes the work visible and gates the leak, it does not do his thinking).

**Test plan:** red -> green. RED today: a check over the live queue reports 202 live tickets with no intention, 150 of them in sprint; and `setstatus <id> sprint` on an intention-less ticket SUCCEEDS. GREEN: the transition is REFUSED with a message naming what is missing and why (no property -> no mutation -> no proof). **Discriminating fixture a hollow build could not pass:** a ticket whose `intention` field is present but EMPTY-ISH (whitespace, 'TBD', 'unknown') must ALSO be refused — a presence-check passes that, a real gate does not. This matters because 'unknown' is exactly what the shipped extractor writes 1,722 times, and it must never be mistaken for an authored intention.

**Proof obligation:** the hollow build is: add the gate, backfill 150 one-line intentions that restate each title, run a green check, declare done. What that CANNOT pass: the whitespace/'TBD'/'unknown' fixture, and — the honest limit, stated per CP1 — **no check can determine whether an intention is TRUE, only whether it is PRESENT and non-degenerate.** Whether the 150 sentences say something real is a human judgement, it is Akien's, and it is precisely why they are drafted for ratification rather than generated.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. The triage->sprint transition REFUSES a ticket with a missing, empty, or degenerate ('TBD'/'unknown'/whitespace) intention, at the cc_queue chokepoint.
  2. The red run (202 live / 150 sprint / intention-less sprint entry SUCCEEDS) is recorded before the green.
  3. The 150 are surfaced as a standing worklist, not a one-off report.
  4. A draft->ratify path exists so clearing them is review, not authoring from blank (depends on T-intent-extractor-agent's drafter).
  5. The count of live intention-less sprint tickets is reported at day-close (calm signal, a number, never an urgency flag) so it cannot silently re-accumulate.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
